titleStmt.title: Letter from Edwin E. Mason to the Under-Secretary, 23 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edwin E. Mason

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Edwin E. Mason, a Mullingar solicitor, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland, submitting his application for the vacant position of Clerk of Crown and Peace for County Westmeath. He outlines his twenty-eight years of professional experience as a solicitor in the county.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5615/10687

correspAction.placeName: Earl Street, Mullingar, County Westmeath, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Earl Street,
Mullingar, 23 June 1916 Dear Sir, I beg to apply for the position
of Clerk of the Crown and Peace for
Westmeath now vacant. I am a Graduate and L.L. B.
of Trin. Coll. Dub. and a practicing
solicitor resident in the County having
my office in Mullingar where I have
carried on a large business as solicitor
and Land Agent for the past 28 years I am solicitor for the County Council
having acted for that Body since its
formation and having previously acted
for the Grand Jury. I have the honor to be
Your obt. Servant Edwin e Mason The Under Secretary
for Ireland
Dublin Castle
